SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description1Txt: Funds to Community Hospital Association "CHA" Fairfax MO -- help purchase MRI. CHA is critical access hospital which provides inpatient outpatient & emergency services to citizens of Atchison Holt & Nodaway Counties Missouri & adjacent areas in Nebraska and Kansas.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description2Txt: Funds to Tarkio-Westboro Community Care Center Tarkio MO "TWCCC" -- help purchase vegetables meat & nutritional food for Atchison County area -- providing food for 508 families and 948 individuals in 2021 -- all who qualify based on income and approved by Community Services. Volunteer based and contribution sustained.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description3Txt: Fairfax United Methodist Church Fairfax Missouri -- provide food and activity funds for children at Eagle Lake Camp & for No Hunger Summer.
SummaryOfDirectChrtblActyGrp.Description4Txt: Individual grants for medical education paid to the educational institutions -- 12 recipients in 2021. The grants are given for individuals within the Atchison County Missouri area to encourage the retention of well-trained competent medical services in this rural area.

ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.FormAndInfoAndMaterialsTxt: Letter detailing the purpose of the grant & amount requested. The purpose of the Trust is to provide funds to enable medically-related services to citizens of Atchison County Missouri. Applicants should state how grant would benefit Atchison County Residents regarding medical services.
ApplicationSubmissionInfoGrp.RestrictionsOnAwardsTxt: Limited to organizations or individuals who are or will be providing medically-related services of any kind to citizens of Atchison County Missouri.

GrantOrContributionPdDurYrGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: Food & activity funding -- Eagle Lake Camp & No Hunger Summer for children

GrantOrContriApprvForFutGrp.GrantOrContributionPurposeTxt: Eagle Lake Camp & No Hunger Summer -- $24,000 over 3 years 2022-2024
Provides advocacy and resources for victims of violent crime and domestic abuse. Provides shelter for victims. Benchmark established by court -- $2,000 year
Sheltered workshop for residents with disabilities Benchmark set by court -- $5000 year
Provides meals to shut-ins and to senior citizens in the Tarkio and Fairfax Missouri areas Benchmark set by court -- $2000 year
Grants for persons seeking medical education -- estimate 12-13 grants year Benchmark set by court -- $6,000 person year
Grant to purchase xray equipment -- payable in 2022
Critical access hospital that provides inpatient outpatient and emergency services to Atchison Holt & Nodaway Counties Benchmark set by court -- $5,000 year
Provides group homes and living assistance for people with disabilities Benchmark set by court -- $5,000 yr
Purchase MRI -- payment over 4 years 2019-2023 -- $25,000 year

CashDeemedCharitableExplnStmt.ShortExplanationTxt: All Trust Grants require the pre-approval of the Atchison County Missouri Circuit Court. The Trust has obtained approval for multi-year grants and continuing grant distributions for a variety of charitable and qualified purposes. These distributions are in addition to any requests to be received and addressed during the tax year. An annual accounting is provided to the Missouri Attorney General reflecting all income expenses assets and liabilities of the Trust. Pre-Approved Multi-year Grants: approx $15,000.00 One recipient with a 3 year grant Continuing Grants Pre-Approved Benchmarks: $80,000 year Seven recipients with varying Court approved benchmark grants of $2,000 to $6000 each per year Estimated Expenses Incurred to Carry out the Charitable Activities: $53,761 including attorney Fees administrative Expenses taxes and Trustee's Fees

CashDistributionExplnStmt.ExplanationTxt: Expenses to carry out charitable activities: Atty fees Admin exp taxes trustees fees

CompensationExplanationGrp.ShortExplanationTxt: Total Trustee Compensation is based on 10% of the Gross Farm Revenues and Investment Revenues and is paid in the following year. Compensation received in 2020 will reflect 2019 revenues.
Total Trustee Compensation is based on 10% of the Gross Farm Revenues and Investment Revenues and is paid in the following year. Compensation received in 2020 will reflect 2019 revenues.

LegalFeesGrp.CategoryTxt: Preparation of Annual Settlements Grain Reconciliations
Prepare Grant Motions Judgments & Related Court Appearances

ReductionExplanationStatement.ShortExplanationTxt: Under the terms of the Last Will & Testament of Raymond Carpenter "Will" which Will created the Raymond Carpenter Trust -- the farmland held by the Carpenter Trust is not to be sold. The farmland is to earn income to support the charitable purpose of the Carpenter Trust. The Will is specific regarding the manner in which the land is to be maintained for example restricting pasture land from being tilled. Because the real property cannot be sold and must be held in trust the value of the real property is discounted to reflect this lack of marketability and inability to tap into the principal of the Trust for any purpose.

TaxesDetail.CategoryTxt: 2020 Real Estate Taxes -- Atchison County Collector
2021 Real Estate Taxes -- Atchison County Collector
